title: Weekly open incident report by saved search
problem: Support leadership wants a repeatable weekly report of open incidents that match defined filters.
approach: Use a saved search, email template, and a scheduled quick action or workflow-driven automation.
steps: Prepare the email template and output fields.
Schedule the action and validate recipients.
outcome: A repeatable operational report is sent automatically without manual effort.

title: Reactivate a ticket on customer response
problem: Tickets in waiting state remain inactive even after the customer replies.
approach: Detect customer response, update status, and notify the assigned team automatically.
steps: Identify waiting states and response channel.
Configure the trigger or automation logic.
Change the record state and notify the owner.
Test repeated replies and edge cases.
outcome: Work is resumed faster and follow-up does not get lost.

title: Multi-step approval routing for service requests
problem: A request must be routed through multiple approvers depending on request type and business rules.
approach: Use workflow branching, approval steps, notifications, and fallback handling.
steps: Define approval conditions and approver logic.
Send notifications and track response timing.
Test approvals, rejections, and no-response paths.
outcome: Request fulfillment is governed and auditable.

title: Integrate Ivanti with an external approval service
problem: An external system must approve requests while Ivanti stays synchronized on outcome and status.
approach: Build a secure REST integration with status mapping, retries, and exception visibility.
steps: Map status values and update rules.
outcome: Approvals stay aligned across systems with traceability.

title: Push scheduled report data to an external endpoint
problem: Operational data needs to be delivered on schedule to another platform for downstream consumption.
approach: Use saved data, mapping, and scheduled API delivery with validation.
steps: Define the source search or dataset.
Handle failures and alert on exceptions.
outcome: Data exchange becomes repeatable and measurable.

title: Respond to a non-compliant mobile device
problem: A device violates policy and must be flagged, restricted, or remediated.
approach: Use compliance rules, notifications, and access or remediation actions.
steps: Notify the user or support team.
outcome: Policy violations are handled consistently and quickly.

title: Roll out a managed mobile app
problem: A new business app must be deployed to managed mobile devices with clear governance.
approach: Target the right devices, validate ownership model, apply the app, and monitor success.
steps: Identify target group and ownership type.
outcome: The app rollout is controlled and easier to support.

title: Set up a BYOD enrollment flow
problem: Personal devices must be enrolled with appropriate privacy and policy boundaries.
approach: Design enrollment, policy assignment, communication, and support expectations around BYOD.
steps: Configure the enrollment path and baseline policies.
Prepare user communication and support guidance.
outcome: BYOD devices are managed with appropriate balance of control and user trust.

title: Deploy patches in phased waves
problem: Patching all devices at once creates unnecessary enterprise risk.
approach: Use pilot groups, staged rollout waves, monitoring, and exception handling.
steps: Create pilot and broader rollout groups.
outcome: Patch rollout is safer and easier to troubleshoot.

title: Targeted software rollout
problem: Software needs to be deployed only to the right device sets with visibility into completion.
approach: Use device targeting, distribution jobs, validation, and exception tracking.
outcome: Deployment reaches the intended audience with measurable results.

title: Create device groups for controlled management
problem: Operations need logical grouping for patching, deployment, and reporting.
approach: Use grouping rules that align to business or technical segmentation.
steps: Build the groups and validate membership.
Use them in patching or software deployment.
outcome: Endpoint operations become more controlled and reusable.

title: Track and plan asset refresh
problem: Aging assets need visibility for refresh planning and ownership review.
approach: Use lifecycle state, inventory visibility, and reporting to identify refresh candidates.
steps: Align discovered data with asset records.

title: Reconcile discovered inventory with asset records
problem: Discovered devices and asset records do not align cleanly, reducing trust in the data.
approach: Normalize key fields, align identifiers, and reconcile duplicates or ownership gaps.
steps: Review source fields and matching logic.
outcome: Inventory and asset reporting become more trustworthy.

title: Redesign a warehouse picking workflow in Velocity
problem: A frontline picking flow is too slow and requires too many manual steps.
approach: Simplify screens, optimize prompts, and design for scan-first execution.
outcome: Operators complete tasks faster with less friction.

title: Simplify a field workflow for handheld use
problem: Field users struggle with a browser flow that was originally built for desktop use.
approach: Adapt the experience for smaller devices, focused actions, and clearer execution steps.
steps: Identify friction points on handheld devices.
outcome: Field work becomes easier to complete accurately on mobile hardware.
